title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Defense · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective cluster lifecycle and recovery defense; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in local clusters, cluster lifecycle, conformance, upgrades, backup, and production defense, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cluster lifecycle and recovery defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.
Trace kubeadm bootstrap, certificates, static Pods, control-plane endpoints, etcd membership, kubelet configuration, CNI installation, join tokens, and high-availability responsibilities.
Evaluate Kubernetes conformance, supported versions, distribution lifecycle, cloud integration, add-ons, feature gates, APIs, operational ownership, and vendor support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Minikube Local Transfer
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.

Inspect the K8s Minikube Local Transfer trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-jskdyr". Tailor its cluster-recovery-defense.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, keep K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-jskdyr
  - competency: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: disaster recovery capstone
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: rendered manifests immutable digest policy and rollout status
    case-key: jskdyr
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone structural check is only one gate. cluster lifecycle and recovery defense must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

At Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone case
Predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, security, disruption, and cleanup effects for the debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone case
Review the smallest manifest, Kustomize, Helm, policy, or runbook increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone case
Reject one failure, record repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable-cluster transfer evidence for the debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone case

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.
steps.why: Retrieving K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone through the concrete cluster lifecycle and recovery defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, keep K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.
